behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior (recursive watching / event propagation from nested paths). MOCK-MISS and REAL-INFRA both apply: in-process faked events can hide misconfiguration where OS events from subdirectories never fire. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text contains no AI/LLM step, only filesystem watching.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon against a temp watched root on disk, create a nested subdirectory, write a file inside it, and assert exactly one transformed output is produced in the output directory for that nested file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text targets deduplication logic for same path + time window, which can be validated at one object/function boundary with controlled clock input; no required OS/network/subprocess boundary is stated. Rejected higher levels: Integration is not required because no explicit module handoff failure is described; System is disqualified because behavior does not require real filesystem events, only request dedup semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup component twice with the same canonical file path under a fake/controlled clock within the configured window; assert first request accepted and second rejected, then advance clock past window and assert acceptance again.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS emitting duplicate events; this is a real infrastructure phenomenon. MOCK-HIDE applies because mocked watcher callbacks may not reproduce platform-specific duplicate event patterns/timing. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by absence of model-dependent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with a real watched directory and perform a single file write pattern known to trigger multiple low-level events (e.g., write+rename/flush). Assert pipeline runs once for that file and only one output artifact is produced.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at OS filesystem lifecycle boundary (delete/recreate inode/path while process remains alive). In-process fakes often miss watcher invalidation/rebind behavior. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because behavior is purely daemon + filesystem resilience.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real temp directory, remove that directory, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and verify it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on interplay of real file metadata timestamps plus watcher/event scheduling under concurrent arrivals. MOCK-MISS risk is high: synthetic ordered callbacks can mask race and timestamp-resolution issues seen on real filesystems. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow disqualified since no AI decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files nearly simultaneously in the watched directory using real filesystem operations, capture daemon processing order (via logs/hooks/output sequence), and assert order matches file creation timestamps with a deterministic tie-break rule if equal.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real write-in-progress timing and watcher/event semantics. Fakes can’t reliably reproduce partial-write visibility and readiness races, so MOCK-HIDE applies. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because there is no model-output dependency, only filesystem/process coordination.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon, begin a long/streamed write to a file in the watched directory (keep descriptor open), verify processing does not start before close/flush, then close writer and assert processing starts and output reflects full file contents.
